⚠️ **Work in Progress**: This library is still under construction and contains **bugs and missing features**. Use in production environments is not recommended.


## Shapes

The following shapes are supported by Pangolin:

##### 0-dimensional shapes:
- [`Point`](#point): A point in the plane.

##### 1-dimensional shapes:
- [`Segment`](#segment): Unoriented straight line segment.
- [`OrientedSegment`](#oriented-segment): Oriented straight line segment.
- [`Line`](#line) Infinite straight line.
- [`OrientedLine`](#oriented-line) Infinite oriented straight line.
- [`Ray`](#ray) Half-line.
- [`Polyline`](#polyline) Open polygonal chain, possibly self-intersecting.
- [`MonotoneChain`](#monotonechain) Weakly x-monotone polygonal chain.

##### 2-dimensional shapes:
- [`Halfplane`](#half-plane) A straight line and all points on one side of it.
- [`Triangle`](#triangle) Unoriented triangle.
- [`Rectangle`](#rectangle) Axis-aligned rectangle.
- [`Disk`](#disk) A circle with its interior.
- [`Polygon`](#polygon) Simple polygon.
- [`Convex`](#convex) Convex polygon.


There are many [predicates](shape_methods.md#predicates) and [other methods](shape_methods.md) supported by all shapes, such as `intersects`, `contains`, `squaredDistance`, `distanceL1`, translation, and scaling.
Shapes may be degenerate, for example when some of their defining points are equal. The behavior of geometric operations on degenerate shapes is undefined. However, degenerate shapes may safely be constructed and are often constructed by the default constructor that sets all points to the origin.

All shapes contain their boundaries (that is, they are closed in the topological sense). The boundary of a shape is the *manifold boundary*, that is:

- A point has no boundary.
- The boundary of a 1-dimensional shape is the set of (at most two) extreme points of the curve. The boundary of a segment are its two vertices. The boundary of a ray is its one vertex. A line has no boundary.
- The boundary of a 2-dimensional shape is defined in the usual way. The boundary of a triangle is its perimeter, the boundary of a halfplane is the line that defines it.


### Point

The [`Point`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html "Two-dimensional point with optional label payload.") class template defines a point with x and y coordinates. A point has no boundary and has the point itself as the interior.

```python
p = pgl.Point(7,9)
```

You can read the coordinates of a point `p` as `p[0]` and `p[1]` or [`p.x()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ad4a835037f85566d87811a0c23716ce1 "Returns the x coordinate.") and [`p.y()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#adddf838c3aab69fee39bf5ffb847f72d "Returns the y coordinate."). You can also iterate through the coordinates.

```python
for coord in p:
    print(coord, end = ' ')
print(p)
# Output: 7 9 (7,9)
```

A point has methods:
- [`p.swapped()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a313ef4ae28a7d9f0405b6dd455775120 "Returns the point with x and y swapped."): Returns the point with x and y coordinates swapped.
- [`p.dual()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a6eff780dd37bb234d722052fa0453f5d "Returns the dual Line."): Returns the dual line $y = ax - b$ for a point $(a,b)$.
- [`p.polar()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#adfb9d9182a567e05d249f6ce4a124f7d "Returns the polar Line."): Returns the polar line $ax + by = 1$ for a point $(a,b)$. Throws an exception for the origin.

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#aa06f32e7c4b67bca6c5496338013d099 "Returns the bounding box of the point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ac6c5f7ac1da90230f66cd728cdbb34cc "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ab2d80a52e632151a84d01fabeee4f9df "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a20acac3c991b9489de40c842430bf1d1 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a0f327863bbabf1c8603aaa16027b39fe "Returns the Euclidean distance to another point."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#af8c141c3ca491737267797594a98f6bd "Returns the Manhattan distance to another point."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#acf57d15ddb1243e56313a0837a850f81 "Returns the Chebyshev distance to another point."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a1e50aa613a7e7816d94ad513ebf12a9e "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#adff20afafe150bcd59564be45d60533a "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to another point."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#affbc8c233a054eb17ef242b80c326a20 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to another point."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a9a3e12195880f93bfe9134fb65e03ff0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == value, or -1 if no coordinate equals value."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ae640aa027e64e99f6fd92376d994ec3f "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a0a19504077318cffd3499793d0ca4489 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a4cf5b7063703e7ef5631f73c1226a416 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a978673d53aef608f7d454587e183ce1d "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a09a86c3fc54499ce0b6e648767718670 "Returns the point rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a5257d2376bd32fd3c62ed63f286f8141 "Returns the point with its x-coordinate divided by a scalar."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#afb57a1fb69e5cc0eee711be0af78d2a6 "Returns the point with its y-coordinate divided by a scalar."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a4ec089864bdfef502ac1ac7bc7f23d5e "Returns the point with its x-coordinate multiplied by a scalar."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#aff72d453142e9fc27ddc5cacf5edd1fe "Returns the point with its y-coordinate multiplied by a scalar."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ab6713425c50817615a734393e7380c55 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a6d56fab92043ca5524419b0a5247f358 "Returns the number of coordinates (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a5fa90525827db2e52afc8302d73f8bf2 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a44d038bcb17b403550c81b3d1c6b46d7 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to another point.").

### Segment

The [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label.") class template defines an unoriented straight line segment. The segment always stores the endpoints in increasing order.

```python
s = pgl.Segment(1,2,3,4)
t = pgl.Segment(3,4,1,2)
if s == t:
    print(s, "==", t)
# Output: (1,2)--(3,4) == (1,2)--(3,4)
```

You can read the two endpoints of a segment `s` as `s[0]` and `s[1]` or [`s.min()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a8ca634e3fec2026ef7a18c4a2652667d "Returns the smallest stored endpoint.") and [`s.max()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a7ed2bf58648e2eb9b29c349b4762aaca "Returns the largest stored endpoint."). You can also iterate through the endpoints.

```python
s = pgl.Segment(3,4,1,2)
for i in (0,1):
    print(s[i], end = ' ')
for p in s:
    print(p, end = ' ')
# Output: (1,2) (3,4) (1,2) (3,4)
```

The interior of a segment is all the segment except the two endpoints.
```python
s = pgl.Segment(1,0,5,0)
t = pgl.Segment(2,0,2,3)
if s.intersects(t):
    print("Intersect!")
if not s.interiorsIntersect(t):
    print("Interiors do not intersect!")
# Output: Intersect! Interiors do not intersect!
```

A segment `s` has methods such as:

- [`s.midpoint()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6ba4f70a48483f85bb36651d9fa16275 "Returns the midpoint of the segment."): Returns the midpoint.
- [`s.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a8c2640b1515b891d3458b89e2bf852ac "Returns the Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].distance(s[1])` as `float`.
- [`s.squaredLength()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ae7e06f6bf41a97e0b9a2e2af80eb442d "Returns the squared Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].squaredDistance(s[1])`.
- [`s.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a2d653f0f6dfd0664e8afe91816e262c1 "Returns whether both endpoints coincide."): Returns `s.length() == 0`.
- [`s.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ad93d1387983c7189e9e201382111c87c "Returns whether the segment is vertical."): Returns `s[0].x() == s[1].x()`.
- [`s.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6903b76ac4686b9335f6f116c53f322d "Returns whether the segment is horizontal."): Returns `s[0].y() == s[1].y()`.
- [`s.containsEndpoint(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a90315b916a47d2356ff1793db49f9d63 "Returns whether the given point is one endpoint."): Returns `s[0] == p or s[1] == p`.
- [`s.collinear(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a98ea3f482d52dec230b1a6ff2832af79 "Returns whether the given point lies on the supporting line."): Returns whether `s` and `t` are on the same line.
- [`s.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6104dac8ebf1ae689aad41031b1c853e "Returns the slope of the segment."): Returns `(s[1].y()-s[0].y()) / (s[1].x()-s[0].x())`.
- [`s.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#af8c2ca1c497f63c2d7fdd9c984201ed2 "Returns whether another segment is parallel to this one."): Returns whether `s` and `t` have the same slope value.
- [`s.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#af4487113eb0a0b25129415b203d39b27 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns the value of the segment y coordinate at the given coordinate `x` or `None`.
- [`s.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#afc0fd6524c9dd5b450e9ca6393df79d9 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns the value of the segment x coordinate at the given coordinate `y` or `None`.
- [`s.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a17e749637dd68e87c00a49b75e80f958 "Returns the supporting line."): Returns the line that contains `s`.

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a447581d0b86d603605ca84856053521a "Returns the bounding box of the segment."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a9e774664af9f26d7e4f01953164ac4c0 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a15fa0d7b15c40acc23e67ac20f8f824f "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a0a120c1369fa8b45230699027e719c56 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac9c887b4cf6891189bc6405920b515e1 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a034374fa22d22920fed05609d8939fd9 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a9603e82a2099e0d085d6f21ade77ee3c "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#aaa49a813cf1e051dd88a3effd0ab57ad "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ab69e99b0ab7483c67c473a3082dba971 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a07c1240cdbdf75152ec3bd381db6cb45 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no endpoint equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#afd75a960b9cf2e02cb4ef55d63574d6a "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a9b47429f4ff8bc31ab74e86777b46026 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a47cf496a356bdc7ceeda6e6cdba548cb "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a1d07afa003d27addae7ec7a0e86f736e "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`lengthL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a5f85458293a2a86ac3727b39a1cb09dc "Returns the Manhattan length."), [`lengthLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a403c0f01a4a06c9a66870d26ee3fbb91 "Returns the Chebyshev length."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac381a6cbc94661fbb3f2e602f3999c78 "Returns a point inside the segment."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a9e5459b86e0eaf6f007d26681a73a240 "Returns the segment rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac4e8d3430d7104397cb7c9986edcd3c2 "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a64ec4d17a0155867dc878685acb99d2c "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a30cf555d792ffdd84c962b05eb54f63a "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a7601f099064cb93721e95d03b2f4371f "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a830c2dec5f515ba822e95e19229565c5 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a889dfc74b64baa67e97bc4c7dd413a6a "Returns the number of endpoints (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a96dd962e8a2361d1cb0bc7a32ee5af48 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ae7bba7fbc2fefc7de46ce8a32a4f4c02 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to another segment."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a08097be84764754c35aac6cc6f1d5d96 "Returns whether one endpoint equals the given point.").


### Oriented Segment

The [`OrientedSegment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html "Directed segment preserving source-to-target order plus optional segment label.") class template defines an oriented straight line segment. The user chooses the order of the two endpoints, which are named [`source`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2ed4278927ccabd73b1d99c8f8903f10 "Returns the source endpoint.") and [`target`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a6ed02b384a6f31fd6b4c8671286f7231 "Returns the target endpoint."), respectively.

```python
s = pgl.OrientedSegment(1,2,3,4)
t = pgl.OrientedSegment(3,4,1,2)
if s != t:
    print(s, "!=", t)
# Output: (1,2)->(3,4) != (3,4)->(1,2)
```

You can read the two endpoints of an oriented segment `s` as `s[0]` and `s[1]` or [`s.source()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2ed4278927ccabd73b1d99c8f8903f10 "Returns the source endpoint.") and [`s.target()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a6ed02b384a6f31fd6b4c8671286f7231 "Returns the target endpoint."). You can also iterate through the endpoints.

```python
s = pgl.OrientedSegment(1,2,3,4)
print(s)
# Output: (1,2)->(3,4)
```

An oriented segment `s` has all methods of the [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label.") class:

- [`s.midpoint()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a9fcc978c0e1b9d9053f2182da496db20 "Returns the midpoint of the segment."): Returns the midpoint.
- [`s.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a003b0ff77fdeaff80dfcd5591f884a8f "Returns the Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].distance(s[1])` as `float`.
- [`s.squaredLength()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a9314e8f0decc3ea85b2ec04a6bb845d8 "Returns the squared Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].squaredDistance(s[1])`.
- [`s.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a4dc0c036962ec7a1a9142296de15e64f "Returns whether both endpoints coincide."): Returns `s.length() == 0`.
- [`s.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a7193013018c0600c23b9719e9a631247 "Returns whether the segment is vertical."): Returns `s[0].x() == s[1].x()`.
- [`s.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ac8af96509cf5232e92b04a02cedcfa46 "Returns whether the segment is horizontal."): Returns `s[0].y() == s[1].y()`.
- [`s.containsEndpoint(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a270cc8f80403c643fd94bc36e9ecff50 "Returns whether the given point is one endpoint."): Returns `s[0] == p or s[1] == p`.
- [`s.collinear(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#aeb0cf7ae8f1880e3671599fd760f458b "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the oriented segment."): Returns whether `s` and `t` are on the same line.
- [`s.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2e104f74709a9c280784291df2e868d0 "Returns the slope of the segment."): Returns `(s[1].y()-s[0].y()) / (s[1].x()-s[0].x())`.
- [`s.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#afba1cca649f1edc93dc94bd69be42ca5 "Returns whether the given segment is parallel to the oriented segment."): Returns whether `s` and `t` have the same slope value.
- [`s.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a8f66de4ed57d7708e522a0bb1f77b566 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns the value of the segment y coordinate at the given coordinate `x` or `None`.
- [`s.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ae08fd372179c6d2b5d146435c2e3abc8 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns the value of the segment x coordinate at the given coordinate `y` or `None`.
- [`s.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#af7700575ec9d0865ab559d3a70b3ab94 "Returns the supporting line without orientation."): Returns the line that contains `s`.


It also has:

- [`s.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#abd0d595b6568119fd0a07a4ef5f4250b "Returns the opposite orientation of the same geometric segment."): Returns the segment with source and target interchanged.
- [`s.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a531355c630b3b171d3e7ab41f6842149 "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the segment."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `s[0],s[1],p` as an `int`: `0` when they are collinear, `-1` when `s` sees `p` to its right, and `+1` when `s` sees `p` to its left.
- [`s.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a16de5b4095b9e58bbd2caa405fe44a7c "Returns the half-plane on the right of the segment direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `s.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`s.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a848ba0978471a2ad0df5e246ecd2d077 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the segment direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `s.orientation(p) >= 0`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- [`s.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a32abd5495bfe740ffcfaead86078214b "Returns the oriented supporting line."): Returns the line that contains `s` and has the same orientation.
- [`s.asRay()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ab9b6246fac158c6af6fc77f20b713a04 "Returns the ray with the same source and direction."): Returns the half-line that contains `s` and has the same source.

- Other methods: [`asSegment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a839015c7197fe69821533cda01d8136a "Returns the segment without orientation."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a27d265989a331167765f44b9d859a6c5 "Returns the bounding box of the oriented segment."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a31c81c01b377355dbfcd9dd4f02f3fad "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#abaafa2a3f5387d07b4797c2933ea4aee "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a20c07ae3def1d7976af11cb18f0e5cbf "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a3b7ea89d712f539e67e785f829d2f6f5 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a90dfd6d9b702d80a2757598fd9f7009f "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a7d84f893e981bf010eeff2e81e4e4d91 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a24eca87c73872ebef717020ba6b87517 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#af6d974417644e3bc7cad31abd71ab70f "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a0e85f32bac2d7392328e751be0a1e1f3 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no endpoint equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#adb5ad6e2277aedfc5c4bc2580b3c1dfe "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a13a2665b2be362ac9107d31b66c83929 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a3d238333c1e855b066903070a70bf830 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#aaf0873655ea8d3d5961e599a6face4ee "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`lengthL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a105f79c0fb325725b753dee91a411857 "Returns the Manhattan length."), [`lengthLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a39e8b93dccbd0e2dcc60cdd19a188e78 "Returns the Chebyshev length."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a5299844de92c345405c81bbe2d499bf9 "Returns the lexicographically largest endpoint."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a1f7b0a29be7c37d9e6cc2070199b0562 "Returns the lexicographically smallest endpoint."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a5a399c0606d7b4641bd10a8c11d85d80 "Returns a point inside the segment."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a37bf7b25137a0712fbfb0b0137c8b0f9 "Returns the segment rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ac8e29df8884ff2f554669a5be3bfca47 "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a542b94b42a0f19c4164239d41a8c638d "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ab82c6757dc55fcc1eb9724011b2cb395 "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ad7d0de7a56972078d47a5efbabe5c4f7 "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a661283a85e835c20cf6624b462f961fc "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a477436b9bbddaa54cd8cc7f6263aa52f "Returns the number of endpoints (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ac13ca0015e4196b5b6ff2f6d2f63dd2f "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a487d135155f37f65f90323e74c131090 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to another unordered segment."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#aa515dbca8dc2332613b1e0267e8516f6 "Returns the two endpoints in source-to-target order."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a247b09048136f7092ba4642d734943d6 "Returns whether one endpoint equals the given point.").


### Line

The class template [`Line`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html "Unoriented infinite line.") represents an infinite unoriented straight line. A line is stored as any two points it contains, but two lines defined by two distinct collinear points always compare equal. The two points are stored in increasing order.

```python
l1 = pgl.Line(1,2,3,4)
l2 = pgl.Line(2,3,1,2)
if l1 == l2:
    print(l1, "==", l2)
# Output: -(1,2)--(3,4)- == -(1,2)--(2,3)-
```

The defining points may be accessed as in a segment. The interior of a line is the whole line, so [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a0f408aa140c9bd52f34b2b7569e3d32d "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B).") and [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ada10a674bd914aa57d211add8a53dfe8 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B).") are equivalent.

A line `l` has some additional methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aea48143a2889d294c15306a18bbc382f "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4b206c7339d9e8dd2c13c41de28f2d12 "Returns whether the line is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a066e7201c620f1ce7311ca130b6bd672 "Returns whether the line is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a65b09754cb121f239f3133a082fbdaeb "Returns the slope of the line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4fba0a6b1449af25c123a338628d8ae5 "Returns whether another line is parallel to this line."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope value.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a68570dbd34b227416c066766c61249ef "Returns the half-plane geometrically above this line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl.OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) >= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aecad497c330e3cfdeac70d0b54bebf3a "Returns the half-plane geometrically below this line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a68570dbd34b227416c066766c61249ef "Returns the half-plane geometrically above this line.").
- [`l.dual()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aa72029be2b77416b240bb2511a8e6e57 "Returns the dual point."): Returns the point $(a,b)$ such that `l` is defined by $y = ax - b$. Throws an exception for vertical lines.
- [`l.polar()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a9a9a9d07c0d3aafcc6b273bc70dfbb0a "Returns the polar point."): Returns the point $(a,b)$ such that `l` is defined by $ax + by = 1$. Throws an exception for lines through the origin.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a7788ca87e20897f7a5a4efe74fd7055c "Returns the y-coordinate of the line at a given x-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a70a94d1e1a7c7bd1108ae00c273fde85 "Returns the x-coordinate of the line at a given y-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

- Other methods: [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a36cd0d13335330379612fbd0a72f0284 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4cfa4e78e3e5b32c03d28c3982d10fad "Returns whether the line interior contains the given point."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a76eec7b766eecde475537034df31286c "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a151fcdf3d89877652115f0aef06ce8d1 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a8a38cba848b1ccad656ed53868cc8517 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aa78d70c2e1b07bee3ba4eba2083c29c7 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#acb7f7b3f4940841503855d09b8947c23 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a8ad8361f30c1fb7b9549826912bd2dca "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#abde7287285f241414eb264250503aeb8 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a51dbd1d954a537292ae236cd5b67aad2 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a320576b5b0950794eb5994b2b32faa03 "Returns the largest stored defining point."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a5143b9b0c107fecab04a397b47203156 "Returns the smallest stored defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a02a7f68895d3822c49025defca738341 "Returns a point inside the line."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a7aeca6a269be181179e5b450f59898a7 "Returns the line rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a38451225a7e1adf9a0f0df5837fcec9a "Returns the line with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#afa660ffc7505164effa8a082cc983059 "Returns the line with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ad729ec6057d0a597cdc40339ab877cce "Returns the line with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a6e276431cbef396ca4c3b23c83d95f09 "Returns the line with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a1c62c1282194fcd3c8004b44c1c27d40 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a87a202d457d89f32911b6dc811be6361 "Returns the number of defining points (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a6fabdb5bd2f6def14eece9e20d295455 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a91b2e25a94740265188b175967c14f8c "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Oriented Line

The class template [`OrientedLine`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html "Directed infinite line with left/right side semantics plus optional line label.") represents an infinite oriented straight line. An oriented line is stored as any two points it contains but the order matters as the line is oriented from the source to the target point. Two lines defined by two distinct collinear points compare equal if the points are in the same lexicographical order.

```python
l1 = pgl.OrientedLine(1,2,3,4)
l2 = pgl.OrientedLine(2,3,1,2)
if l1 != l2:
    print(l1, "!=", l2)
l2 = l2.opposite()
if l1 == l2:
    print(l1, "==", l2)
# Output: -(1,2)--(3,4)-> != -(2,3)--(1,2)->
#         -(1,2)--(3,4)-> == -(1,2)--(2,3)->
```

The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment. The interior of an oriented line is the whole oriented line, so [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a197af38d52a8dec41b6f3d0850e8a7ef "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B).") and [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9b9427e23d27587f59c32a60dfd6e8ba "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B).") are equivalent.

An oriented line `l` has methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ab0a5530262e75a3f25423ea3f5bf14cd "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#add58c7c5ce15d56d0d142e7bc145d581 "Returns whether the line is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aed8a8d6c00d0366a910bc72a1f646040 "Returns whether the line is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9bf9b108074f26a0943b5d4cc34d8a67 "Returns the opposite orientation of the same geometric line."): Returns the oriented line with source and target interchanged.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a31868e82121d74b3016766b5482ebff6 "Returns the slope of the line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af3e4eb23abc25f758b37cb1f5d70a286 "Returns whether the given line is parallel to the oriented line."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope value.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aeda2dc46e413a0d34b5cb2a127a29ba5 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl.OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) >= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad35665c61b605d8b0057ae774367d7d6 "Returns the half-plane geometrically below the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aeda2dc46e413a0d34b5cb2a127a29ba5 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line.").
- [`l.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aabcb4586ac2a8cc5c655eaa9f2208bca "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the line."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `l[0],l[1],p` as an `int`: `0` when they are collinear, `-1` when `l` sees `p` to its right, and `+1` when `l` sees `p` to its left.
- [`l.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a5b450be0b6c1ebcee8d26b805ba000fb "Returns the half-plane on the right of the oriented line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`l.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a3d80b03525b963c2255216fb6769ddd0 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the oriented line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) >= 0`.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad7299b16efd66c8a5e620684c240a96e "Returns the y-coordinate of the supporting line at a given x-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9066c3e2a39cb45346775fe4f985ef72 "Returns the x-coordinate of the supporting line at a given y-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#abb932f7bbe6ceb52cc031c12d8c4558b "Returns the line without orientation."): Returns the line without the orientation.

- Other methods: [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a2e52ea375be1fb13b67e0256f7945ac0 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a65120372dbe5b2486ae04039b984472a "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the oriented line."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a0458bbebfaaa1841bf90a90cded19564 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#acf5ee045bcc3eef55541dcd73577c966 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ac383baef8ff418c69958b32deab46209 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af8f8a1643b96fa06d90612e56bd73541 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ab8976011bb7580e03e4811d42935209d "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ac0870879c32a5911c0702fb303c1b5c5 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a5accc11603e7e8048e70ccd16d6c3f13 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad5eebc41bc419dccadbc6e63cdc09f30 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a981d03ccc8abb5b6daebcd90b61c4a45 "Returns the lexicographically largest defining point."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#adeebd042e968ec518a76dbe1cda3f8c9 "Returns the lexicographically smallest defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a3c73c7f60b7910bc45c49daec553312b "Returns a point inside the oriented line."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#abc039c9ba303040c5dd4376d542e1f84 "Returns the oriented line rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a611619ece83ec021f58300ec3b73cb2d "Returns the line with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9ee00df6878f79419aeb38a0a2de818a "Returns the line with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aa6b67ae0ee9c92e6d6ddcecbd12438c7 "Returns the line with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a2bb4f7a97b0ad36090de7546b04a03e6 "Returns the line with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a2b979efcc57a28163049ef34e2adf895 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a7a41d4c4b6949741f4186f5916d9cf03 "Returns the number of defining points (always 2)."), [`source`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#acb02ae1912f2c8f784bd3613860a8f93 "Returns the source defining point."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a7f4b131a180458db2bdcd2b04c44d106 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`target`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a54e4382e43dee0d9b39e20a84c949cd2 "Returns the target defining point."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a7a6c854b76f6e0eadf672dee09b30091 "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Ray

The class template [`Ray`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html "Half-infinite line starting from one source point plus optional ray label.") represents a half-line. A ray is stored as its source endpoint and any other point it contains. Two rays `l1`,`l2` are equal if they have the same source and the other defining point of `l1` is contained in `l2`.

```python
l1 = pgl.Ray(1,2,3,4)
l2 = pgl.Ray(2,3,1,2)
if l1 != l2:
    print(l1, "!=", l2)
l2 = l2.opposite()
if l1 == l2:
    print(l1, "==", l2)
# Output: (1,2)--(3,4)-> != (2,3)--(1,2)->
#         (1,2)--(3,4)-> == (1,2)--(2,3)->
```

The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment and may be changed directly. The boundary of a ray is its source.

A ray `l` has methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a68014d94d2e314877bf4a09aaf39ad39 "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a05e1dd210bf5c5c3ec92adfceb6d5fa5 "Returns whether the ray is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa75a93acd72c880836337a5b2bae3b9f "Returns whether the ray is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a749a6d7a25334d7d452c06c939a1ef24 "Returns the ray obtained by swapping the two stored defining points."): Returns the ray with source and target interchanged.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a93ea798d1e9270ced1a7450346fda9b1 "Returns the slope of the supporting line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a31d03810caf016b8b5d4dcc8af4ad61d "Returns whether the given line is parallel to the ray."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope value.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a00aedab9d7a8015114914fd415645177 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl.OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) >= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa8ccb101750ad8b384d3637e9ff74a76 "Returns the half-plane geometrically below the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a00aedab9d7a8015114914fd415645177 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line.").
- [`l.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0cb4c142550a83fb2a46205e3c566bd4 "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the ray."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `l[0],l[1],p` as an `int`: `0` when they are collinear, `-1` when `l` sees `p` to its right, and `+1` when `l` sees `p` to its left.
- [`l.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0a101de661262dab7e4bc1e48dffd724 "Returns the half-plane on the right of the ray direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`l.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a01083ffe650f8b9ffaa393f7574f6005 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the ray direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) >= 0`.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aca8c3ed33fcaafeb3a212cc37b1c51b7 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns the ray's y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`, or `None` if the ray has no point there.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a1da16a6f7958862040effa675515dc07 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns the ray's x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`, or `None` if the ray has no point there.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae47f5294685f87b606737e49cf1982f9 "Returns the supporting line without orientation."): Returns the line containing the ray.
- [`l.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a714c4d79f71316abc554f9b5f832b197 "Returns the oriented supporting line."): Returns the oriented line containing the ray and the same orientation.

- Other methods: [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa8fb539f32ed24a3b4ab72249754004c "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a2cd767b07ca2dd0f031dd7679c68d632 "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the ray."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae0a3ac3654f116edc50c0b26e6838115 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a54b58104cdb1a3bef2fd04d3fcbb1876 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae4f6c732976aca9b3a4d263f7f8a5664 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ab9d136bb4dc01963dac4be687e2f477d "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a22ddc33b71c1a6893470a05d65a87059 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a3e9100b021385db17bd0f7e3c010830a "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0af762abf9afa1c69da436b7e14afba6 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a9dd77e5daffa10f9cf3980a79b84cfb3 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a64baaf5c8110942301642a63fbb8de25 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ac9a371022edbeb66f2c6b7dc1407cc98 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a7d70e6c27e28e6f2fcc50cd4efcecb5b "Returns the lexicographically largest stored defining point."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ab0cd668afb5c0cf698edcf702d700e2e "Returns the lexicographically smallest stored defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#adf32c22c527e752a545bbe33573f331c "Returns a point inside the ray."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#af3781a8fa9b18651c3c8639b43eef8a0 "Returns the ray rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#add5fb1793af3b1d2b3acc6a8b6d9118a "Returns the ray with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a1c97d5ce403e7087ef660f070540fec3 "Returns the ray with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#acedbd446825c16383884a8b71d8a6b77 "Returns the ray with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a684a08d5881224203c5291ba58ee2d81 "Returns the ray with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#afe62a3bac7131dd6ac281020453c4e59 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ac04b3f1567932d63175fa45e26627f86 "Returns the number of defining points (always 2)."), [`source`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aec703bff01d5b109827dd64f5aa234de "Returns the source point of the ray."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ab9096d1c3a423afa8750b796eafc5744 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`target`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a82a8c7d0e1b35b770ea4f9f2efe67224 "Returns the second stored point defining the direction."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a89cc49900b6085bdb013a80a2c009023 "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Polyline

The class [`Polyline`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html "Open polygonal chain stored in traversal order; may self-intersect.") represents an open polygonal chain: a sequence of vertices joined in traversal order, with $n - 1$ edges for $n$ vertices and no closing edge back to the first vertex. Unlike a [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices."), it may cross itself.

The constructor keeps the vertex order you give it, canonicalizing only the *direction* (the sequence is reversed when the reversal compares lexicographically smaller), so a polyline equals its own reverse but not a different traversal of the same vertices:

```python
p = pgl.Polyline([pgl.Point(0, 0), pgl.Point(2, 2), pgl.Point(2, 0)])
print(p)
# Output: Polyline[(0,0),(2,2),(2,0)]
print(p == pgl.Polyline([pgl.Point(2, 0), pgl.Point(2, 2), pgl.Point(0, 0)]))
# Output: True  (the same chain, traversed backwards)
```

Like [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices.") and [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices."), a polyline stores a lazy translation, so translating it is $O(1)$; it is mutable and therefore unhashable.

A polyline `p` has methods such as:

- [`p.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a109775a30d3c17acb2b610e5139a17ee "Tests whether the polyline is simple (it does not touch or cross itself)."): Returns true if the chain does not touch or cross itself. A *closed* polyline (last vertex equal to the first) is not simple.
- [`p.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a3c7ecb999d35c0430c7ec57468f9d9d1 "Checks if the polyline is degenerate (all vertices are equal, so it covers at most a single point)."): Returns true if every vertex coincides.
- [`p.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a9317b71453005525d3e6d20309478119 "Computes the Euclidean length of the polyline (the sum of its edge lengths)."): Returns the Euclidean length (a `float`: a sum of square roots is irrational in general).
- [`p.lengthL1()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aa07788cbfbca38496e4920d9a6cbc827 "Computes the Manhattan (L1) length of the polyline.") / [`p.lengthLInf()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6a8eb393f3ee27522fc9de7d3601475a "Computes the Chebyshev (LInf) length of the polyline."): Return the exact Manhattan / Chebyshev length.
- [`p.pointInside()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aac18f99afe9c98f8da82d38f804cbe70 "Returns a point inside the polyline."): Returns an exact point in the relative interior (the midpoint of the first edge).

As a 1-dimensional shape, its boundary is its two extreme vertices and its relative interior is everything else — the same convention as [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label."). [`p.intersection(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a615fbb18098d3774821bd2b932203c02 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") returns a *list* of [`Point`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html "Two-dimensional point with optional label payload.") and [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label.") pieces, since a chain can meet even a line in arbitrarily many disjoint places. It is not defined against [`Disk`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html "Closed Euclidean disk stored by boundary points plus optional disk label.") or [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices.").

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a0f6e26a628acedb87aada9fb34159c89 "Computes the bounding box of the polyline."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aa4d5c3d3cf700d00e22da815a4a426de "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a8d0533b6ff156b2ee349a702b9d6c403 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aebb135e0f5c547fe7f5706cdca8d93a1 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad58e4c2e63c54448fa1cda4f0025f67f "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad11916b51735271db1644e7aed3269bc "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a06f8935547ebe27b5d33c270c5f80c94 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ab6a4485c970a76a715ac8cdb53a3c3ae "Returns the edges of the polyline."), [`empty`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aaf09aab7866b1c11308bfc2ec48ebd41 "Checks whether the polyline has no vertex."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a88d8370a57e89159083f7f67218d1a9c "Accesses a vertex by index modulo the vertex count."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6127f4d2249823ad30b95e1ace380c8e "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if point is not a vertex."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#afb0ee9bea14097783cb307615f483a34 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a95b79e8265b13ad3b5568ae0f6785684 "Tests whether the interiors of the shapes intersect (A° ∩ B° ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a9e4508c932fc0bb5f9bc054e9b0634a2 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a7e28c494881b86066d5a0ae1452ad341 "Returns the oriented edges of the polyline, each directed from vertex i to vertex i + 1 in traversal order."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a2cc3afdb5d34613bb633ef042ba1ec3e "Rotates the polyline by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a88d76f3590d6368f2dbdacc6d6cfb318 "Returns the polyline rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a76e813756a16eb766d514001346d9f20 "Divides the polyline's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aea07da15eeea6a9c900fb42a6cb9fde8 "Divides the polyline's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a5f576177d6edeecc1013c710ebe96629 "Multiplies the polyline's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a108c436aad4d816e3a7dde7e3b64dc93 "Multiplies the polyline's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a965b73578bf22e03bbfba04fe7fe199b "Returns the polyline with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ae1afefbc2850a63d6976ffa11397ba05 "Returns the polyline with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a8dc5b5f26dc605638e7850c9b2973a68 "Returns the polyline with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#adeac1628802f5cbb817ab0bcceb1f6c9 "Returns the polyline with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a2732dc06ae36d3f65d3f6b554bd02e76 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad621eb6c07a174dba0275b3ac9cdb982 "Returns the number of vertices in the polyline."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aed0a0c982b916cd1bb2930192f08cfdc "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a5521a7f3ebcda9cfc23f918477728cd2 "Returns the vertices of the polyline (translation applied).").


### MonotoneChain

The class [`MonotoneChain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html "Weakly x-monotone polyline stored by lexicographically sorted vertices.") represents a weakly x-monotone polygonal chain. Its vertices are stored sorted lexicographically (smaller x first, ties broken by smaller y), which means the constructor treats its input as a **point set**, not as a pre-linked chain: the points are sorted and deduplicated, so any input order yields the same chain, and the chain is automatically simple.

Consecutive vertices may share an x-coordinate, producing a vertical edge — that is what makes it only *weakly* monotone.

```python
c = pgl.MonotoneChain([pgl.Point(2, 2), pgl.Point(0, 0), pgl.Point(1, 3)])
print(c)
# Output: MonotoneChain[(0,0),(1,3),(2,2)]  # sorted, whatever the input order
print(c.isStrictlyMonotone())
# Output: True  # no two vertices share an x, so the chain is the graph of a function
```

The sorted order buys $O(\log n)$ vertical queries, which no other shape has. Each returns `None` when the query x lies outside the chain's x-extent:

- [`c.indexAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a393d5b3cc782e79a7036ee3beb72257f "Locates the vertex or edge of the chain at a given x-coordinate."): The index of the vertex at `x`, or of the vertex starting the edge spanning `x`.
- [`c.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9f8a70bee1528bc6809112b9916fae85 "Evaluates the y-coordinate of the chain at a given x-coordinate."): The exact y-coordinate of the chain at `x`.
- [`c.isBelow(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a4bc4bcb2b9210d4d4d320556798ee343 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly below a point.") / [`c.isAbove(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9eaf22f78515682edac8b3b1590615f1 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly above a point."): Engaged when a ray shot straight down / up from `p` hits the chain. A point *on* the chain satisfies both.
- [`c.isStrictlyBelow(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ab4111e9c8705101090ac65c78f108feb "Tests whether the whole chain lies strictly below a point at its x.") / [`c.isStrictlyAbove(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a1dea6225dd09e3ef817b8d348852b326 "Tests whether the whole chain lies strictly above a point at its x."): The strict variants. A point on the chain satisfies neither.

It is also the only chain that can grow: [`c.insert(point)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5cd1ec77da22ad3d957149d51e3d8a05 "Extends the chain to contain the given point as a vertex.") or [`c.insert(points)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5cd1ec77da22ad3d957149d51e3d8a05 "Extends the chain to contain the given point as a vertex.") splices new vertices into the sorted sequence (a duplicate is ignored).

Otherwise it behaves exactly like a [`Polyline`](#polyline): $n - 1$ edges, `Segment`'s boundary convention, a lazy $O(1)$ translation, mutable and therefore unhashable, `length`/`lengthL1`/`lengthLInf`/`pointInside`, and a list-valued `intersection` that is not defined against `Disk` or `Polygon`.

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a8dac08a30c2d04f9fed863e81f8ba4cf "Computes the bounding box of the chain."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5a5cc531b6bc964dbc4ee96e404aae87 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5ca0039a7ceff302c988f0ee2fca3226 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ac70944fe81641ed674b8a30e44f246c1 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a216d0b9490b2e2805ff7caa35950a14e "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9b90faf34bce85e9bca85890240ba658 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a055799497d4803c75814af39f0770a41 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a92e90f9bcdbfc6e904af497d5663da47 "Returns the edges of the chain."), [`empty`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a68a879bf1a5ace95eceee472e5ac975e "Checks whether the chain has no vertex."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#add88d1347c3116ad64f2dc4409f09e6a "Accesses a vertex by index modulo the vertex count."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a53da56f4e34cd40d265dfcb5c0ae324e "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if point is not a vertex."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a6b5febb331d9b109b10c1b86eddcbeb0 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5012f6515614d82ab910aacb9c9c6925 "Tests whether the interiors of the shapes intersect (A° ∩ B° ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#acf0c387012c4d665ac17e0c3fecec81b "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a3ff7a150d0eff57b3538d5375c299cec "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`isDegenerate`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a7eb13f8c59d5d0798e3e15bfca4739a6 "Checks if the chain is degenerate (fewer than two vertices, so it has no edge)."), [`isStrictlyMonotone`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ad6166871233a0625fdd3ce18f00b2f5c "Tests whether the chain is strictly x-monotone."), [`length`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#aeeb80f55a73ab440b6e119e02827ee74 "Computes the Euclidean length of the chain (the sum of its edge lengths)."), [`lengthL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#afca60f5968fff4ff8e6a1d8c38aeeaa3 "Computes the Manhattan (L1) length of the chain."), [`lengthLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a6b0f1704432d4fff888a8cd935a499f9 "Computes the Chebyshev (LInf) length of the chain."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ad166e8b9f7fe4d131afae894547eeab5 "Returns the oriented edges of the chain, each directed from the lexicographically smaller to the larger endpoint."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a966eceea67acc6d9b2c2defbfda803f7 "Returns a point inside the chain."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#abf239362929ccb24f652dbf9f7f8d279 "Returns the chain rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a50453d1e844bdff4076d321ce916a5f3 "Divides the chain's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#af0596914afdedc7cc304390302ca03a6 "Multiplies the chain's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a85818dbb3df86154c6a37d0b988b3a14 "Returns the chain with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."),
