### Half-Plane

The class template [`Halfplane`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html "Closed half-plane defined by an oriented boundary line.") is stored as an oriented line, but represents a completely different geometric object that contains all points on its left half-plane. The boundary of the half-plane is the line that defines it. Two half-planes are equal if the corresponding oriented lines are equal:

```python
h1 = pgl.Halfplane(1,2,3,4)
h2 = pgl.Halfplane(2,3,1,2)
if h1 != h2:
    print(h1, "!=", h2)
# Output: ^-(1,2)--(3,4)-^ != ^-(2,3)--(1,2)-^

h2 = h2.opposite()
if h1 == h2:
    print(h1, "==", h2)
# Output: ^-(1,2)--(3,4)-^ == ^-(1,2)--(2,3)-^
```

Halfplane does not have an `intersection` method. The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment and may be changed directly.

A half-plane `h` has methods such as:

- [`h.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ae962754a791826b988b39273a9e0da7e "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `h[0] == h[1]`.
- [`h.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a86a553f8847f65c8e89fe1a612ffcfe3 "Returns whether the boundary line is vertical."): Returns `h[0].x() == h[1].x()`.
- [`h.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ae4b2bac2a079079c00870916a7c93b0f "Returns whether the boundary line is horizontal."): Returns `h[0].y() == h[1].y()`.
- [`h.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a4bcdbe04bd9d5f266d6c1bdf0bc2ad9e "Returns the complementary half-plane with reversed boundary orientation."): Returns the half-plane with source and target interchanged.
- [`h.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#aea8e2495979f8da401683ed157578993 "Returns the slope of the boundary line."): Returns `(h[1].y()-h[0].y()) / (h[1].x()-h[0].x())`, possibly negative.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ade5884b09f45d35c46a09f9cf90c32b6 "Returns the boundary line without orientation."): Returns the line bounding the half-plane.
- [`l.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ade8b1566d820ead8ec0d5865c2d46d3c "Returns the oriented boundary line."): Returns the oriented line bounding the half-plane.

- Other methods: [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#abdafb4b982b7a9ae9fdbc587c0bb8503 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#aa856d60bab5d2988ac376a3d15a0453e "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ab94d5650f2cc974f8a123ce29bec2bd0 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a680fd9297fdcaf1f06c60ec3f37e6a38 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a371048d4b3a901d1e06ff0f3e799a3df "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a77cfc9646566571ab9271b538d71132c "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a27001adb22631bfa0a96debd380e5ee0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a295982b670d6e2fed6bea923f846ba2d "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a3c0a7a96fd0c8501342ca7b1474e4e6d "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a84b7430e17ab5198cfaa11058075ccc4 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a4362ec5ee1c67c386760f7c32a6c3746 "Returns the lexicographically largest defining point."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a547f9125924efa0946219f00fcee2a77 "Returns the lexicographically smallest defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a27a12a9125ffbaa3462d19ae663382c0 "Returns a point inside the halfplane."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a02da3e0f6aa4cbe728626e123cbdcb84 "Returns the half-plane rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#abd9b49407239781913144a75d6b0e65a "Returns the half-plane with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a066142b9746ea170f4f3f99c2698e36f "Returns the half-plane with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#acdbf1d581ad0e02e1d26385489a58797 "Returns the half-plane with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#afed416d1e7064ca97fab9b2bb516ae89 "Returns the half-plane with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#aea0ac54c623c9a2d7adf0a6614000a4e "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a3a2b16706b1386ddf56f4d5d80a25ec4 "Returns the number of defining points (always 2)."), [`source`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ae7c0c337ad387486eddbb86422c58951 "Returns the source boundary point."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a53f6f3e77acc41f565449202012df44c "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`target`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a9d1ab9c6b15908dc9fd95d94250fc1c6 "Returns the target boundary point."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a7d8d6875b24c6dfacd894926105c97fa "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Triangle

The class template [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices.") is stored as three points, called vertices, which are kept in the following order. The first vertex is the smallest lexicographically and the other two vertices are ordered such that the triangle is oriented counterclockwise (positive orientation test). Two triangles are equal if they have the same vertices.

```python
t = pgl.Triangle(3,3,4,1,1,1)
print(t)
# Output: <(1,1)(4,1)(3,3)>
for i in {0,1,2}:
    print(t[i], end=' ')
# Output: (1,1) (4,1) (3,3)
for p in t:
    print(p, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1) (4,1) (3,3)
for s in t.edges():
    print(s, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1)--(4,1) (3,3)--(4,1) (1,1)--(3,3)
for s in t.orientedEdges():
    print(s, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1)->(4,1) (4,1)->(3,3) (3,3)->(1,1)
```

A triangle `t` has methods such as:

- [`t.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ab159614e587f6dc4a75789f98fb7a848 "Tests whether the three vertices are collinear."): Returns true if there are equal vertices or all vertices are collinear.
- [`t.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#af601a145a531947ed2b4475ab0102f78 "Returns the arithmetic centroid."): Returns the centroid.
- [`t.circumcircle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a0b9e50f4c10a634c4706884ff2894fb7 "Returns the circumcircle of the triangle."): Returns the circumcircle.
- [`t.isRectangle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a4cfb7d8fffa11293343c535bc4825e3f "Tests whether the triangle has a right angle."): Returns whether one angle is 90 degrees.
- [`t.isObtuse()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7848d637384e479a97395489bf9a61fb "Tests whether the triangle has an obtuse angle."): Returns whether one angle is greater than 90 degrees.
- [`t.isIsosceles()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a3cc30d928e1cb21c99eae9765f38e3d4 "Tests whether two sides have the same length."): Returns whether two sides have the same length.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl.Polygon) t` or [`t.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#aaa078dd16798d8d58bb3cb46f64aba3b "Returns the triangle as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the triangle.
- `(pgl.Convex) t` or [`t.asConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ab01b4c98a0859edd28f1961012ff1447 "Returns the triangle as a convex polygon."): Returns the convex polygon representation of the triangle.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7d9d7443fe0e474c2a40cc81eeda5aa3 "Returns the non-negative area of the triangle."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a9b3e0556b074ae291d5db07c827463c9 "Returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the vertices."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7b7065114ca1f45e385600fb5fcb6530 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a14847b8bcb9746337a5dc06ce8a1209b "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7ff30d2b4490e2f5d8ba3204e71337f6 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a523e4d5e777d1977ab0a71295a59c9b2 "Returns a segment defining the diameter."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a3bef47d1427200ec9986e1ec624393e4 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ae625954b9a5a27fa38f416e0ce02e57c "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#add3cd93bb69d662b90418d601d36f3f5 "Returns the three unoriented boundary edges."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ac1c69cde482a51d9fa1cb3701338b81d "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a9433d699516983bf40b3cd2739793f73 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ad3f377044212b157346b922721f162d1 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#aca6b47e468bac538594b1f12432b5a03 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no vertex equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ae61d8d98e6a9061de284d50cdab60ffc "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a603a05dcc8b220b156a90c5c15dd611c "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#abc23ecdde98e6499ceec621e3199e032 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a94138aece9a14e3681a7a5d02cfc623b "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#acd5f859d0052fb38ebcaed8238e5d18c "Returns a point inside the triangle."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#adf035881d22ca6cbe87c2af5a28e0dbd "Returns the triangle rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a5c14ce53a281874b080b3679945b453c "Returns the triangle with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a949e61159e8d89fe44d6cd85b6180f0c "Returns the triangle with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a6b8d79ef1c6501429650576156f80daf "Returns the triangle with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#aae145ec80872f51cca79136655c8f7dc "Returns the triangle with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#adc35890e2e4cc6a26f4df737700d8690 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a15b6971531e1e67cdeb401480c2ea086 "Returns the number of vertices (always 3)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a72c15fc35b4f18e175a859b8ff89bb45 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a3609f1585186e4ac26cb6d74af609a8d "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a8ba4cd745c67787ef7209dcb18ff1d09 "Returns twice the area of the triangle."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a395af097b8902119b6c810f516211534 "Returns the vertices in canonical order."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a083e324686e1905ee26cd84ee373c7ef "Tests whether a point equals one of the vertices.").


### Rectangle

The class template [`Rectangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html "Axis-aligned rectangle stored by minimum and maximum corners.") represents an axis-aligned rectangle. While it is stored internally as only two vertices (minimum and maximum x and y coordinates), it behaves as a polygon with four vertices. It can be constructed from two opposite corners (given as two [`Point`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html "Two-dimensional point with optional label payload.")s or four coordinates), from an iterable of points (it computes their bounding box), or from an iterable of bounded shapes — even of mixed types — in which case it computes the bounding box enclosing them all. Unbounded shapes ([`Line`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html "Unoriented infinite line."), [`OrientedLine`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html "Directed infinite line with left/right side semantics plus optional line label."), [`Ray`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html "Half-infinite line starting from one source point plus optional ray label."), [`Halfplane`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html "Closed half-plane defined by an oriented boundary line.")) have no bounding box and are rejected.

```python
r = pgl.Rectangle([pgl.Point(1,3),pgl.Point(2,4),pgl.Point(3,1),pgl.Point(5,4),pgl.Point(2,3)])
# Same as pgl.Rectangle(pgl.Point(1,1), pgl.Point(5,4)) or pgl.Rectangle(pgl.Point(1,4), pgl.Point(5,1))
print(r)
# Output: [(1,1),(5,4)]
print(r.min(), r.max())
# Output: (1,1) (5,4)
for i in {0,1,2,3}:
    print(r[i], end=' ')
# Output: (1,1) (5,1) (5,4) (1,4)
for p in r:
    print(p, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1) (5,1) (5,4) (1,4)
for s in r.edges():
    print(s, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1)--(5,1) (5,1)--(5,4) (1,4)--(5,4) (1,1)--(1,4)
for s in r.orientedEdges():
    print(s, end=' ')
# Output: (1,1)->(5,1) (5,1)->(5,4) (5,4)->(1,4) (1,4)->(1,1)
```

A rectangle `r` has methods such as:

- [`r.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a380388f13b2cd9a5ad8f4f806e31be84 "Returns whether the rectangle has empty interior."): Returns true if the rectangle has null area.
- [`r.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a553634d0d486ae85ec22c6a036855f0e "Returns the centroid of the rectangle."): Returns the centroid.
- [`r.circumcircle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a96f720c89b9116686118e6085374a7ab "Returns the circumcircle of the rectangle."): Returns the circumcircle.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- [`r.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af850885808c25c90aaa04506cc1f1537 "Returns the rectangle as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the rectangle.
- [`r.asConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a9c20bb08763579e119ea85d68a907ab3 "Returns the rectangle as a convex polygon."): Returns the convex polygon representation of the rectangle.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af8e8f46b3461676c7e40a14b6c4ff86c "Returns the rectangle area."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ae88798364c0bf20cd4d14c259fa33dcd "Returns the bounding box of the rectangle."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ab6df84e85e6d33ff9212783b62dd98c9 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ad43d090a0253e4f66e8f73f6288e0fcc "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ade1e0e5f80655722aa51c65e37e08e82 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af310096576e07b538fc1b3eb2ee2f74e "Returns a segment defining a diameter."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ac07f544b22bb10991bf113774e8564e5 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a8935a3e41149260d3dca1fac6cf69939 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a7017ee8e082661250fdcdf9948033f71 "Returns the four edges as unordered segments."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a04908c335b96990b5bd8b6f68ac06299 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ac9ccb7b76729a0de0d31a78b5b855962 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a10f025f38e5b2ba331663d022bdbe595 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#aa27f92baa8430b0fa73726f64f68839c "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no corner equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a93c3696d6f189661db23533b81423c46 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a694706752e3fd0d0771740610c3c519e "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#adbbcf17cdeb0c512f9ee285839219299 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a48a11005af2ba50ec962e81a252b2b39 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ac2c5d9b26f67491dbd62fc700954d08b "Returns the maximum corner (max x, max y)."), [`midpoint`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a36738ba8e4e131ab97cdb2e9fdbccfaf "Returns the midpoint of the rectangle."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a58c52150645f5830ec93a87add23abcd "Returns the minimum corner (min x, min y)."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a3d3a71320826ad5060436195accf8916 "Returns a point inside the rectangle."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a0f7c4ec9134c624403e63118b940a03d "Returns the rectangle rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a555f5bd1891b9a9d0bb0f31daa882eae "Returns the rectangle with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a830f85ca3e30c0e23f276e7e784dfa33 "Returns the rectangle with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#aafd1ad0aae1e6240589726b76dc497dc "Returns the rectangle with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af28a600cb5502bf2702561f2df981c8d "Returns the rectangle with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#afee97fe097d4ce29e7c43885b9214535 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a99388dcbdc773cc434910a7e37600346 "Returns the number of corners (always 4)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a7cff28a98d441e3e25c3beb1cefe66d9 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a15ef653c720d714779abbfbb1990ffc3 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to another rectangle."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a926a8acf69c73483a45f9cd60fc8ec6c "Returns twice the rectangle area."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a8e890106e82852c58aaa51ceff095275 "Returns the four vertices in counterclockwise order."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a0d982fcbd82c865bddc0ab1277aff7eb "Returns whether a point is one of the rectangle vertices.").


### Disk

The class template [`Disk`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html "Closed Euclidean disk stored by boundary points plus optional disk label.") represents a circle with its interior. Disks are stored internally as three boundary points, in the same way as a [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices."). This choice may be surprising, as the standard representation for disks is a center point and a radius. The main motivation is that the circumcircle of a triangle may be represented exactly for integers. Nevertheless, the constructor accepts both forms:

```python
d1 = pgl.Disk(pgl.Point(1,1), pgl.Point(2,5), pgl.Point(4,3))  # Disk from 3 points
d2 = pgl.Disk(pgl.Point(2,3), 4)             # Disk from a point and a radius
print(d2)
# Output: Disk((-2,3)(6,3)(2,7))  # Output always uses 3 points
```

Disk does not have the [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aa9af5f0f6a0a4d6d6139d6c3697a3abd "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") method and cannot be scaled on a single axis. A disk `d` has methods such as:

- [`d.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a19e32c8b7656bfe02982c8a40ea0ff57 "Returns whether the three boundary points are collinear."): Returns true if the points are collinear or equal.
- [`d.radius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a69765084e501902a81583032fdb7c816 "Returns the center in this disk's exact coordinate type."): Returns the radius length.
- [`d.squaredRadius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a0027c4ab0d85b7ff3e5b0d5b42b1745f "Returns the squared radius in an explicitly chosen result type."): Returns the squared radius.
- [`d.center()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a01f21dd5b971164474843df4ad71bfc1 "Returns the center (circumcenter of the three boundary points) in an explicitly chosen coordinate type."): Returns the center point.
- [`d.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ad7c86ba7778cf349bcb6c653e0470ba5 "Returns a segment defining a diameter of the disk."): As always returns a diameter [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label."), but for disks the segment is always horizontal.

- Other methods: [`a`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aab80a3c4810748f39fdc8c7e020eb232 "Returns the first boundary point (lexicographically smallest)."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aa4f2837f14655fd73098513f769e41da "Returns the squared radius in this disk's exact result type."), [`b`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aeb247fff089ba4d4463418f84f0f9e3d "Returns the second boundary point in canonical order."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a82543add1cf92f0b0116fd798ddecced "Returns an axis-aligned bounding box in the coordinate type."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a190ee43500b52f235991dc9bc3095180 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`c`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a20d60679286b84a64063b648c860430e "Returns the third boundary point in canonical order."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a40627a86d5eb68250e7daba7a1ca36db "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a3afd10decc72c4a11747024bee9c635b "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a569e24bcd39f95c5901e956fd1ea70d7 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance from this disk to a point."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a0c438698a94776de27b7738d325066f3 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance from this disk to a point."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#afd129f4cb8111846762e2b4ca06db7c1 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ab6e63f207efba3178e1a665c70f60cd0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no boundary point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a5c43e8fa09ae818be5fa70d2f18c9474 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a7932e3c1de11bf765ca378582df887e6 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a04a545293c1496c34b4bf601a124eff6 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a78477a3bcf69c6f36cff63e5a65442e3 "Returns the midpoint of the first two boundary points."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a07288ad0eb805ea8a8e6a67d5b8e497c "Returns the disk rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ae384cc53e8754c9618a33a6a0d58945c "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a9276993379a1097fb9a2c3464faf66ed "Returns the number of stored boundary points (always 3)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a714b6843c9d986d4da9776c33528cf47 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance from this disk to a point.").


### Polygon

The class template [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a simple polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container that must be given in the order they appear on the polygon. The vertices are accessed in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y). ~~Internally, the polygon is stored as multiple x-monotone polylines for improved performance.~~

A polygon `P` has methods such as:

- [`P.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af1d297b10aee661458543c2f5fa00645 "Checks if the polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the polygon has null area.
- [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a5e182cf106f6be5ed3fb49c07daf80c6 "Tests whether the polygon is simple (its boundary does not touch or cross itself)."): Returns true if the edges only intersect at the endpoints of consecutive edges. Takes $O(n \log n)$ time for $n$ edges.
- [`P.isConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a4fd2d656a5e668f8679db56d2d7d5257 "Tests whether the polygon is convex."): Returns true if the polygon is convex, possibly with vertices subdividing convex hull edges. Takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`P.untangle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ab85503d835bc7afa41dedd1140caa1dc "Makes the polygon simple in place by uncrossing its boundary."): Makes the polygon simple in place, returning `None`. Edges that cross are flipped, and when a flip is blocked by collinear vertices the offending vertex is removed instead, so the vertex set may shrink. On return [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a5e182cf106f6be5ed3fb49c07daf80c6 "Tests whether the polygon is simple (its boundary does not touch or cross itself).") holds. Worst-case complexity is high.
- [`P.pointInside()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a01d3ae4bdc2b2e560f0c6e4c9e95b78d "Returns a point strictly inside the (simple) polygon."): Returns an exact point strictly inside the polygon, even a non-convex one (the vertex average would not do: it can fall in a notch, outside the polygon).
- [`P.triangulation()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a55c6ba6ed18502faa4cebbe7e15c60ae "Builds the constrained Delaunay triangulation of this polygon.") / [`P.triangulation(segments)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a55c6ba6ed18502faa4cebbe7e15c60ae "Builds the constrained Delaunay triangulation of this polygon."): Returns the constrained Delaunay [`Triangulation`](data_structures.md#triangulation) of the polygon, optionally with extra constrained edges.

`P` is not convex in general, so it has neither a Hausdorff distance nor
`verticesContain` (use `P.index(point) is not None` for the latter).

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad4765e3f6390f06a59f25fef4fcb219f "Computes the area of the polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a1ff93373098baf44e68ba00bb02565d6 "Computes the bounding box of the polygon."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a27e7ae09110e8b6d3a0c3b18885d9302 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`centroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a66951f26f6ab36fa08fc6bd0188d3234 "Computes the area-weighted centroid of the polygon."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a3a46d062f49f67213b208549e06dd23c "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a2b23c4c3144eb5a06d76effe28e000e2 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a34513cb8bcf668d052f7d15ad58cfe85 "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a56659a4fdc5fcb61d01fcf63214da66d "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae4e6f284d1992e5344831a13dd8e011d "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad8456b5d296bc23059b0ffe7053dd74d "Returns the edges of the polygon."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae81b4e6f9129d731d6732f8b20bd45db "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end. Useful for iterating polygon edges where the last edge wraps around."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a4242053f262a712d1e38cc5ced8e301b "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if point is not a vertex."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a28a4948401349722af11f2d72259c08f "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#abdbbc8bf65fe9ec40cf0e6bc6dcecb1a "Tests whether the interiors of the shapes intersect (A° ∩ B° ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a77ae5b1d8b5e653fc0d369515e1c46c8 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a18a02370ff879f41588d11fdbd26fa91 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ab6c6a1c32666c9f220ba03c2c4864f98 "Returns the oriented edges of the polygon."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#abae5b3272afe3c706ff1378fb6d00683 "Rotates the polygon by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae737bfc7fb10dc9fbe6f9101dad4edc6 "Returns the polygon rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#afcc813c8d7e00a28290e5e535de205d7 "Divides the polygon's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a8161a2a6e3c75c8eb5f3b6ca0659e9f4 "Divides the polygon's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a7485d53c8396b8402102695e9f122255 "Multiplies the polygon's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#abdd5508e61d4c797f02257116e2d3125 "Multiplies the polygon's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#aeeff4e7fc91d48475d65b9d7bb263733 "Returns the polygon with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a74f9e5944239271b44cf12a4fdc6788a "Returns the polygon with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae6f150525d7140e673bcd0089816d38f "Returns the polygon with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a3578b5ad99b36b49160c36bdfdf82b8d "Returns the polygon with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af5ef3e311c9b72c159e20de09fe8c8ef "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9a70b70146923bd677bebf7ee9ef38b8 "Returns the number of vertices in the polygon."), [`verticesCentroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af868c2942503c2bafaf89f64af1fe463 "Computes the centroid of the vertex set (the average of the vertices).").


### Convex

The class template [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a convex polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container and will construct the convex hull. The vertices are stored in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y). If the container already has the vertices in order, a second constructor parameter can be set to true to avoid computing the convex hull.

A convex polygon `c` has methods such as:

- [`c.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a460c2cbe73f75e60d0153be2acb1662d "Checks if the convex polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the convex polygon has null area.
- [`c.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad4000c6649317b13e2695a209d9102e8 "Computes the centroid of the convex polygon."): Returns the centroid.

It knows how to convert itself to:
- [`c.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ab08ccc569a03b8056db708b1f4e26e2f "Returns the convex polygon as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the convex polygon.

If the convex polygon `c` has $n$ vertices, then:

- [`c.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad44a3c0e434930328c723fcae6c4c7c9 "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair).") takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`c.intersects(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`s.intersects(c)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`c.intersects(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\min(n+m) \log(n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.
- Other predicates take the same time as [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).").
- [`c.intersection(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a56ad5f95e9f45d5f00af38f50470c7ea "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") takes $O((n+m) log (n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a75a11db621ea33f8ea69f4946de7034e "Computes the area of the convex polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a06e8f55ebcb8cf814bbc5df5d4288312 "Computes the bounding box of the convex polygon."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#afd902656ee3c6a53a741ba7325248f0e "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#abbd6d5e37f4cb4ac7c3af412fe851ad3 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a650d1e04ed66ea74364cd7ca974879ff "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#af463ad4527581d3110e4f9827e3dafb8 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a3f1688a4a47dc9804cf0937899446b13 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a9dcf1c9ee107a02ec9893e2baa59620c "Returns the edges of the convex polygon."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a20a4c5a95a53f91958478a8f40908e0e "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end. Useful for iterating polygon edges where the last edge wraps around."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a06f907f788b5c3568694a688b7436134 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a22644989a9d97a2d4447762ae2a23a08 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a66ca8f0c5c1fb858dc88134abe664207 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a15719a7622fa3025b9eef141dbfceb61 "Returns a point inside the convex polygon."),
